preregistered_criterion: a concrete resolution threshold vs diffusion length
verdict: RESOLUTION THRESHOLD EXISTS and SCALES with diffusion length: the null absorbs the structured boundary residual only when its resolution is finer than h* ~ 0.7-0.9 ell, and h* GROWS with the diffusion length L_D (a longer diffusion length smears the boundary signature to a coarser scale, so a coarser null already absorbs it). Sec 7.2's weak-vs-strong-null claim is resolution-driven and now quantified: strong null := resolution < h*(L_D). With metabolism the residual is structured and the threshold is meaningful; WITHOUT metabolism the structured residual is 20x smaller (boundary spectrum collapses toward the smooth background), so no resolution recovers structure -- exactly the role Sec 7.2 gives metabolic expenditure in keeping the memory kernel from collapsing.
